title: brain sync [--dry-run] — --json success output
description: Reconcile the vault working tree against the SQLite projection by per-note content hash (v2, #329). There is NO HEAD cursor — the projection's per-note content_hash IS the cursor. `sync` scans + hashes every note file, classifies it against the projection matched by stable frontmatter id into changed / new / dropped / moved, then reindexes changed+new (LanceDB write then projection fold), purges dropped across both stores invisibility-first (SQLite row + identity keys + note_links in one transaction, THEN LanceDB vectors), and updates file_path in place for pure moves (no re-embed). An unchanged tree (clean or dirty) is a structural noop (noop:true, exit 0) with NO index write — the orphan-vector sweep belongs to `index rebuild` alone. `sync` and `sync status`/`status` consume the SAME reconcile routine, so they can never disagree about the pending set. reindexed = changedCount + newCount is derivable (consumers sum), not stored. NOTE: `command` is retained (contract-lint binds every Phase-5 schema's properties.command.const to the registry row); the six flat count/noop fields are the v2 payload.

scannedCount.description: Distinct vault note files scanned + hashed this run (the reconcile input size).

changedCount.description: Notes whose content hash changed (same stable id). Carries the current path — a rename-plus-edit is counted here (file_path updated AND re-embedded once), never in movedCount. Part of the reindexed set.

newCount.description: Vault notes whose stable id is absent from the projection. Indexed fresh. Part of the reindexed set.

droppedCount.description: Projection notes whose stable id is absent from the vault. Purged cross-store invisibility-first (SQLite row + identity keys + note_links in one txn, then LanceDB vectors). Never archived.

movedCount.description: Notes with an identical content hash but a relocated path (same stable id). file_path updated in place, NO re-embed. Never counted in reindexed.

noop.description: true iff changedCount + newCount + droppedCount + movedCount == 0 — a structural noop (the tree matches the projection). A noop run performs NO index or projection write.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Reconcile the vault working tree against the projection by per-note content hash (no HEAD cursor): scan+hash+classify into changed/new/dropped/moved, reindex changed+new, purge dropped invisibility-first, move-in-place pure moves; an unchanged tree is a structural noop with no index write.

flags.description: Scan + classify only: report the counts, mutate no sink (no LanceDB write, no projection fold, no purge). noop still reflects whether the tree matches the projection.

sideEffects: reindex (LanceDB write then projection content_hash fold) for changed+new notes, when not --dry-run and not a noop
cross-store purge of dropped notes: SQLite (notes row + note_identity_keys + note_links) in one transaction, then LanceDB vectors — invisibility-first
in-place file_path update in the projection for pure moves (no re-embed)
prohibitedEffects: any index or projection write on an unchanged tree (structural noop)
an orphan-vector sweep (that belongs to index rebuild alone — a sweep here would break the structural-noop invariant)
archiving a dropped note (v2 purges; derived state is disposable, re-derivable from Markdown)

errorCodes.when: the vault repo is missing/unreadable, or an in-glob note fails to parse (fail-closed — a transient parse error must never be mistaken for a drop)
another maintenance op holds the vault-maintenance lock
an index or projection write failed; the projection content_hash is not advanced past a note whose embed failed, so the next sync re-detects and repairs it
